summary: Backend-only regression + new education feature testing. 37/37 pytest tests pass (23 baseline preserved from iteration_1 + 14 new tests for education gating and members CRUD). All requested backend scenarios covered: member CRUD with clean JSON (no ObjectId leak), admin-only guards, mirror sync between education_members and User.education_access, /api/articles/{slug} gating for egitim category across 4 auth states (no-auth, unapproved writer, admin, approved writer), auth/me exposes education_access, related endpoint unaffected, category list still shows egitim publicly without content_html.

minor.issue: Views counter is NOT incremented when the response is restricted (early return before $inc). Likely intentional (don't count blocked hits) but worth confirming — could also be argued that a request is still a request. Non-breaking.

Uses ?enabled= query param (bool via Query). Works but inconsistent with role toggle which also uses query params — OK. However enabled=false silently no-ops when user has no email match. Non-breaking.
Email validation is only `'@' in email` — accepts strings like 'a@b'. Consider Pydantic EmailStr. Non-breaking.
slugify underscores, writer publish policy, related returns [] on missing slug, bearer logout — none re-tested this run beyond regression pass.

critical_code_review_comments: server.py grew to 937 lines — consider splitting into routers (auth, articles, admin, education, uploads) before it hits the 1000-line threshold.
_is_education_article checks both category_slugs (list) and legacy category_slug (single). Confirm all seed/create paths write both fields consistently — mixed schema is a smell.

toggle_user_education and add_education_member both mutate user.education_access, but there is no transactional guarantee: if the education_members insert succeeds and the users update fails (network blip), state drifts. Acceptable for MVP but worth a comment.

Restricted egitim response still leaks metadata (title, excerpt, cover, author) — this appears intentional (teaser) but should be documented so the frontend team doesn't accidentally expose sensitive fields.
get_current_user returns None on expired/invalid — the gating relies on `not user` which is correct, but any endpoint that does `Optional[User]` gating needs the same pattern. Consistent so far.
test_credentials: Ephemeral MongoDB-seeded users + user_sessions with TEST_ prefix (admin, writer_blocked, writer_approved). Auto-cleaned per module.
seed_data_creation: Module-scoped fixtures create admin (education_access=true), writer_blocked (false), writer_approved (true). Per-test ephemeral users used for grant/revoke mirror tests. Existing seeded 'Opsiyon 101' article in category 'egitim' used for gating tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend fully green including education gating + members CRUD. Frontend NOT tested this iteration (explicitly skipped). Next iteration should run Playwright against: (1) NFinansBadge visible bottom-right on all routes, (2) /kategori/egitim EducationGate for unauth, (3) Article page gate when restricted=true, (4) Dashboard 'Eğitim Üyeleri' tab admin-only CRUD via data-testid='edu-email' / 'edu-add-submit' / 'edu-del-{id}', (5) UsersTab per-user toggle-edu-{user_id}. Seed a session cookie/localStorage using the same mongosh pattern as backend fixtures.
